meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: SaaS Pricing & Fixing Version Control Issues
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Learn how to implement robust SaaS pricing models while solving version control chaos using Antigravity and Claude Code as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: To eliminate version control headaches, implement a Git-based workflow orchestrated by Claude Code. Simultaneously, stabilize your revenue by integrating a tiered SaaS pricing model. By automating your deployment pipeline with Antigravity, you ensure that every code change is tracked, versioned, and tied directly to your production-ready subscription logic.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, sweating because your latest client push just overwrote the entire billing module. Without version control, you aren't just building a SaaS; you're playing Russian Roulette with your reputation. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a high-ticket consultant is the ability to ship reliably without chaos.

We are going to bridge this gap. By combining the infrastructure automation of Antigravity with the intelligent refactoring capabilities of Claude Code, you will transform your development process. We’ll secure your codebase with versioning and wrap your features in a professional SaaS pricing architecture that keeps your clients paying on time.

steps.title: Initialize Repository with Atomic Commits
steps.description: Before writing pricing logic, we must establish a source of truth. We use Claude Code to scaffold a repository that enforces semantic versioning.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a new git repository with standard branching strategies for a SaaS project, including .gitignore for node_modules and environment variables.

steps.title: Define SaaS Pricing Architecture
steps.description: Map your pricing tiers to your codebase structure to ensure your billing logic is decoupled from your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular pricing service that handles Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers, integrating with a mock Stripe API interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor pricing logic into a dedicated module'

steps.title: Automate Versioning with Antigravity
steps.description: Configure your environment so every deployment automatically tags the commit, preventing the 'lost code' scenario entirely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an automated deployment pipeline that tags every successful build with a semantic version number and pushes to protected main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure git-hooks for automated tagging'

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging for Tiers
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to feature flags so you can deploy code without exposing premium features to non-paying users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a feature flag middleware that checks user subscription status before rendering premium components in the UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement subscription-based middleware for protected routes'

steps.title: Stress Test with Claude Code
steps.description: Simulate a version rollback to ensure that your pricing database remains consistent during deployment failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that simulates database state during a failed deployment to ensure subscription data integrity.

pro_tips: Always use 'conventional commits' to generate changelogs automatically; clients love seeing progress reports generated from your commit history.
Never hardcode your pricing logic in the frontend; always validate subscription status on the server-side middleware to prevent bypasses.
Use Claude Code to write your README files; a well-documented repo makes you look like a senior architect even if you are just starting.
Implement a 'staging' branch that mirrors production; never push directly to main without a peer-review cycle.
Set up automated email alerts in your Antigravity pipeline to notify you immediately if a deployment fails, saving you from client complaints.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your version control is locked, integrate a real payment gateway like Stripe using the same modular patterns you established here to start collecting your first subscription payments.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping like a pro. With your version control secured and your pricing logic automated, your SaaS is finally ready to scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
